sources.sourceName: OpenStax Psychology 2e, Ch. 3 Review Questions (Biopsychology) and Ch. 4 Review Questions (States of Consciousness)

sources.sourceName: OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (answer key for odd-numbered review questions)

meta.notes: Psychology (PSBB) items scraped verbatim from OpenStax Psychology 2e Ch. 3 and Ch. 4 review-questions pages; correct answers from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), odd-numbered review questions only. Live OpenStax pages show CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 plus a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing/non-AI per PRD (mirrors psbb-001).

questions.stem: A(n) ________ is a sudden, permanent change in a sequence of DNA.
questions.explanation: A mutation is a sudden, permanent change in a DNA sequence; mutations are an important source of genetic variation.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 3 Q1 = D.

questions.stem: ________ is the field of study that focuses on genes and their expression.
questions.explanation: Epigenetics studies gene expression: how genes are turned on or off, often in response to environmental factors, without altering the underlying DNA sequence.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 3 Q3 = C.

questions.stem: The ________ receive(s) incoming signals from other neurons.
questions.explanation: Dendrites are the branchlike extensions of a neuron that receive incoming signals from other neurons.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 3 Q5 = D.

questions.stem: Multiple sclerosis involves a breakdown of the ________.
questions.explanation: Multiple sclerosis involves the breakdown of the myelin sheath, the fatty insulating layer around axons that speeds neural conduction.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 3 Q7 = B.

questions.stem: Our ability to make our legs move as we walk across the room is controlled by the ________ nervous system.
questions.explanation: Voluntary control of skeletal muscles, such as moving your legs to walk, is governed by the somatic nervous system.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 3 Q9 = B.

questions.stem: The central nervous system is comprised of ________.
questions.explanation: The central nervous system (CNS) consists of the brain and the spinal cord; everything else is part of the peripheral nervous system.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 3 Q11 = D.

questions.stem: The ________ is a sensory relay station where all sensory information, except for smell, goes before being sent to other areas of the brain for further processing.
questions.explanation: The thalamus is the brain's sensory relay station; all sensory information except smell passes through it before being routed to the cortex.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 3 Q13 = D.

questions.stem: A(n) ________ uses magnetic fields to create pictures of a given tissue.
questions.explanation: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) uses strong magnetic fields to produce detailed images of body tissues, including the brain.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 3 Q15 = B.

questions.stem: The two major hormones secreted from the pancreas are:
questions.explanation: The pancreas secretes glucagon and insulin, the two major hormones that regulate blood glucose levels.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 3 Q17 = D.

questions.stem: The ________ gland secretes epinephrine.
questions.explanation: The adrenal glands, located atop the kidneys, secrete epinephrine (adrenaline) as part of the body's stress (fight-or-flight) response.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 3 Q19 = A.

questions.stem: The body's biological clock is located in the ________.
questions.explanation: The body's biological clock, the suprachiasmatic nucleus, is located in the hypothalamus and regulates circadian rhythms.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 4 Q1 = C.

questions.stem: ________ cycles occur roughly once every 24 hours.
questions.explanation: Circadian cycles are biological rhythms that recur on a roughly 24-hour cycle, such as the sleep-wake cycle.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 4 Q3 = B.

questions.stem: Growth hormone is secreted by the ________ while we sleep.
questions.explanation: Growth hormone is secreted by the pituitary gland, with much of its release occurring during deep sleep.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 4 Q5 = C.

questions.stem: ________ is a hormone secreted by the pineal gland that plays a role in regulating biological rhythms and immune function.
questions.explanation: Melatonin, secreted by the pineal gland, helps regulate biological rhythms (including the sleep-wake cycle) and immune function.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 4 Q7 = B.

questions.stem: ________ is(are) described as slow-wave sleep.
questions.explanation: Slow-wave sleep, dominated by low-frequency, high-amplitude delta waves, corresponds to NREM stage 3 (which in older schemes was split into stages 3 and 4).
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 4 Q9 = C (Guide answer text 'stage 3 and stage 4' reflects the older staging; option C is the current 2e wording).

questions.stem: Symptoms of ________ may be improved by REM rebound.
questions.explanation: Per the source, symptoms of depression may be affected by manipulations of REM sleep. The keyed answer is depression.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from the live OpenStax page (stem reads 'REM rebound'); the Student Solutions Guide prints the stem as 'REM deprivation'. Both editions key the answer to C = depression. Flagged for the stem-wording discrepancy.

questions.stem: ________ is loss of muscle tone or control that is often associated with narcolepsy.
questions.explanation: Cataplexy is a sudden loss of muscle tone or control, often triggered by strong emotion, and is commonly associated with narcolepsy.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 4 Q13 = C.

questions.stem: The most common treatment for ________ involves the use of amphetamine-like medications.
questions.explanation: Narcolepsy is most commonly treated with amphetamine-like stimulant medications that promote wakefulness.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 4 Q15 = D.

questions.stem: ________ occurs when a drug user requires more and more of a given drug in order to experience the same effects of the drug.
questions.explanation: Tolerance occurs when a person needs increasing amounts of a drug to achieve the same effect that smaller doses once produced.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 4 Q17 = C.

questions.stem: ________ refers to drug craving.
questions.explanation: Psychological dependence is characterized by an emotional, motivational craving for a drug, distinct from the physical symptoms of physical dependence.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 4 Q19 = A.

questions.stem: ________ is most effective in individuals that are very open to the power of suggestion.
questions.explanation: Hypnosis is most effective for people who are highly open to suggestion (high in hypnotic suggestibility).
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 4 Q21 = A.

questions.stem: Meditation may be helpful in ________.
questions.explanation: Meditation has been linked to benefits for both pain management and stress control, so 'both a and b' is correct.
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax Psychology 2e Student Solutions Guide (CC BY 4.0), Ch. 4 Q23 = D.
